title: Systems and methods for personal internet of things network local routing policy control
abstract: Systems and methods for personal internet of things (loT) network (PIN) local routing policy control are discussed herein. In some cases, an application function (AF) interacts with a core network (CN) of a wireless communication system via network exposure function (NEF) Trafficinfluence service to request local routing through the CN for PIN elements (PINEs) of a PIN (e.g., for PIN elements with gateway capability (PEGCs) of the PIN). In some cases, subscription information for one or a group of UEs of the PIN (e.g., PEGC(s) of the PIN) may be provided that can be used to configure and control local routing through the CN as between those PINEs of the PIN. The local routing through the CN makes the transport of the data within the PIN and across the CN as between two PINEs (e.g., PINEs connected to the CN through different PEGCs of the PIN) transparent to those PINEs.
claims:
Claims 1. A method of a unified data management (UDM) entity of a core network (CN), comprising: receiving, from a network function (NF) consumer of the CN, a request for group subscription data for a group of user equipments (UEs); and providing, to the NF consumer, the group subscription data for the group of UEs in response to the request; wherein the group subscription data for the group of UEs comprises a personal internet of things network (PIN) identifier for the group of UEs and an indication of whether a PIN identified by the PIN identifier is allowed to use local routing through the CN. 2. The method of claim 1, each UE in the group of UEs is a PIN element with gateway capability (PEGC) of the PIN. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein the NF consumer is a policy control function (PCF). 4. The method of claim 1, wherein the NF consumer is a session management function (SMF). 5. A method of a unified data management (UDM) entity of a core network (CN), comprising: receiving, from a network function (NF) consumer of the CN, a request for personal internet of things network (PIN) subscription data for a user equipment (UE); and providing, to the NF consumer, the PIN subscription data for the UE in response to the request; wherein the PIN subscription data for the UE comprises a PIN identifier for the UE and an indication of whether a PIN identified by the PIN identifier is allowed to use local routing through the CN. 6. The method of claim 5, wherein the UE is a PIN element with gateway capability (PEGC) of the PIN. 7. The method of claim 5, wherein the NF consumer is a policy control function (PCF). 8. The method of claim 5, wherein the NF consumer is a session management function (SMF). 9. A method of a policy control function (PCF) of a core network (CN), comprising: receiving, from a unified data management (UDM) entity, an indication of a personal internet of things network (PIN) that uses local routing through the CN; and installing, in a user equipment (UE) of the PIN that is in communication with the CN, a UE route selection policy (URSP) rule that causes the UE to use a unique protocol data unit (PDU) session for the PIN. 10. The method of claim 9. wherein the URSP rule is an only URSP rule of a set of all URSP rules of the PCF that is associated with a route selection descriptor (RSD) that corresponds to the PIN. 11. The method of claim 9. wherein the UE is a PIN element with gateway capability (PEGC) of the PIN. 12. A method of a session management function (SMF) of a core network (CN), comprising: receiving, from a unified data management (UDM) entity, an indication of a first personal internet of things network (PIN) that uses local routing through the CN; receiving, from a user equipment (UE) of the first PIN, a protocol data unit (PDU) session modification requesting to use a PDU session for the first PIN for traffic from of a second PIN; and rejecting the PDU session modification request. 13. The method of claim 12, wherein the UE is a PIN element with gateway capability (PEGC) of the first PIN. 14. A method of a policy control function (PCF) of a core network (CN), comprising: receiving, from an application function (AF), a request for local routing through the CN for data traffic between a target set of user equipments (UEs) identified by the request, wherein the target set of UEs are associated with a personal internet of things network (PIN); and 

 establishing, in response to the request from the AF, a policy and charging control (PCC) rule that causes local routing through the CN for the data traffic between the target set of UEs. 15. The method of claim 14, wherein the request is a network exposure function (NEF) traffic influence request. 16. The method of claim 14, wherein the target set of UEs are PIN elements with gateway capability (PEGCs) of the PIN. 17. The method of claim 14, wherein the target set of UEs is identified in the request using internet protocol (IP) addresses of the target set of UEs. 18. The method of claim 14, wherein the target set of UEs is identified in the request using generic public subscription identifiers (GPSIs) of the target set of UEs. 19. The method of claim 14, wherein the target set of UEs is identified in the request using a PIN identifier for the PIN of the target set of UEs. 20. The method of claim 14, wherein the target set of UEs is identified in the request using an external group identifier for the target set of UEs. 21. The method of claim 14, wherein the target set of UEs is identified in the request using a combination of a data network name (DNN) and a single-network slice selection assistance information (S-NSSAI) that is assigned to the PIN of the target set of UEs. 22. The method of claim 14, wherein the request includes a field that indicates for local routing through the CN for the data traffic between the target set of UEs. 23. An apparatus comprising means to perform the method of any of claim 1 to claim 22. 24. A computer-readable media comprising instructions to cause an electronic device, upon execution of the instructions by one or more processors of the electronic device, to perform the method of any of claim 1 to claim 22. 25. An apparatus comprising logic, modules, or circuitry to perform the method of any of claim 1 to claim 22.
